san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including TV, online streaming, and phone. The agenda focused on making the meeting accessible to everyone, including offering live translations in over 50 languages.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessibility options for residents
No public comment period announced for this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city postponed its zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25th. If you had plans to discuss property zoning issues with the city, you'll need to wait until April 8th instead when the next hearing is scheduled.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects. The group approved notes from their previous February meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support housing and human services in the city.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding (CDBG) available for housing and community projects
Review of applications from organizations seeking development funds
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss ways to support local businesses and get more people shopping locally. They talked about a program to help businesses get their licenses in order, plans to promote local shops during the summer, and ways the city can partner with private companies to sponsor community events and offer incentives for retail stores and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get compliant with licensing requirements
Summer of Celebrations campaign - promoting 'shop local' to residents
Public-private partnerships for event sponsorships - the city working with companies to fund community events
Economic incentives for small businesses and startups - potential tax breaks or grants
Outreach to shopping centers - directly engaging with retail businesses in Cupertino

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they addressed several postponed items and heard from the public on two main issues: a plan to remove weeds from properties around the city as part of a county-wide program, and how federal housing grant money will be spent in the next fiscal year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program - city will remove overgrown weeds from properties
Federal housing grants (HUD funds) - public input on how $$ will be used for housing in 2026-27
Multiple agenda items postponed or reconsidered

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements: one allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and another to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for three years at about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
